_comment: Shared accept/reject fixture for a NORMALIZED YouTube handle, asserted from both sides so the two independent validators cannot drift apart: TypeScript isValidNormalizedYoutubeHandle (src/utils/youtube.ts, src/utils/youtube.parity.test.ts) and Rust is_valid_youtube_handle (src-tauri/src/utils/validation.rs). The frontend normalizes free-form input before it reaches either check, so every entry here is already in the stored @name / channel|c|user/id shape. Add a case to both sides at once by editing this one file.
